title: [Fuchs corneal endothelial dystrophy treated with Descemet's stripping without endothelial keratoplasty (DWEK): a case report].
journal: [Zhonghua yan ke za zhi] Chinese journal of ophthalmology
abstract: A patient with chief complaint of bilateral progressive visual impairment was diagnosed with Fuchs corneal dystrophy (FECD) in both eyes, macular hole in the left eye, and intraocular lens eye in the right eye. After communication with the patient and his family, they agreed to receive Descemet's stripping without endothelial keratoplasty (DWEK) in the left eye. One year after the operation, the cornea of the left eye was transparent, and the central posterior elastic layer was missing about 4.0 mm diameter. The naked vision of left eye was 0.1, and the correction was not improved. It can be seen that DWEK surgery has a good therapeutic effect on patients with mild FECD.
